scope: The derivation pipeline and index lifecycle: cold-rebuild identity, build_id determinism, structural integrity at index time, and spec index. Belongs here: cold-rebuild equivalence (delete .spec-engine/ yields an identical result); build_id determinism for identical inputs; the index-time structural integrity checks; spec index itself. Does not belong here: spec check's diagnostic severity semantics (→ CHCK) or the SPEC.json envelope shape (→ SCHM). Boundary case: cold-rebuild identity is an INDX promise even though the model it rebuilds is defined in SCHM.

requirements.statement: Deleting the derived index and rebuilding from the spec location plus repo tags produces an identical result; nothing lives only in the DB.
requirements.why: Canonical truth is the git spec location — the DB is derived and owns nothing.

requirements.statement: Structural integrity (DUP_ID, BROKEN_SUPERSEDE, BAD_STATUS) is validated against the parsed spec at index time, never enforced as DB constraints.
requirements.why: The DB is not the source of truth; constraints there would imply it owns the spec.

requirements.statement: spec index walks the spec location plus member repos and (re)builds the derived SQLite index, reporting build_id and row counts.
requirements.why: Local multi-repo indexing is the substrate every other command reads.

requirements.statement: The tag scanner finds @spec tags anywhere in a line, derives implements/verifies from the file path, and recognizes the optional unit/integration/e2e level token.
requirements.why: Tags are how code claims a requirement; kind-by-path keeps tags one token long.

requirements.statement: The indexer flattens a requirement's cites field into term_citations edges and resolves each citation by TERM id or by exact term name/alias via term_aliases, landing an unresolvable citation with a null term_id.
requirements.why: Requirements should cite canonical glossary terms; resolution reuses the verified relates pipeline (flatten to a derived edge table, resolve names via the aggregate alias map, no FK).
